segments.text: Okay, look at this. Two different
brands, exact same stripes. [music] Why?
My friend wanted to buy Onitsuka Tiger
because they were trendy. But then I
looked down at my running shoes and
realized I am wearing the exact same
stripes. One is a luxury fashion brand.
The other one is a hardcore running
brand. It feels like a cheap copyright.
Like one of them stole the design, but
actually they're from the same person.
In 1949, Mr. Onitsuga started Onitsuka
Tiger to make basketball shoes. These
stripes were not just a logo. They were
the straps to support your [music] foot.
But as they grew, they merged with two
other companies to form a giant
corporation called A6. They focused
purely on performance and technology.
But people were still missing the old
vintage designs. They did not want a
high-end foam. They wanted the classic
design instead. So AS6 bought the
original name back just for their
heritage line. So AS6 [music] is the
athlete and Onitsuga Tiger is the cool
grandfather. For more design secret
subscribe.
